segments.text:
CELL MEMBRANE
Separates cell interior from environment but
can’t just be a wall
needs to allow nutrients in and waste out

SELECTIVE PERMEABILITY
Small molecules cross membranes at different rates
Rate varies with size and charge

MEMBRANE TRANSPORT PROTEINS
Allow transport of ions and polar molecules across membrane (both directions)
3 classes
channels
pumps
transporters

CHANNELS
Pore formed for passage of molecules
Always passive transport (does NOT require energy) = facilitated diffusion
Ions/molecules move WITH gradient
Many are gated – can open and close

CHANNEL SPECIFICITY
Opening too small for large ions
Channel interactions not possible for “too small” ions

PUMPS
Use ATP energy to move ions/molecules AGAINST gradient
Active transport
Gradient

PUMP EXAMPLE
Na+/K+ ATPase
Moves 3 Na+ out of cell and 2 K+ into cell (per ATP hydrolyzed)
Both against their gradients

JENS CHRISTIAN SKOU
Discovered sodium potassium pump
1997 Nobel Prize

TRANSPORTERS
Uniporter – one molecule
Symporter – 2 molecules in same direction
Antiporter – 2 molecules in opposite directions

UNIPORTER
Conformation change (one face open at a time)
Passive transport
Slower than channel

COUPLED TRANSPORT
Conformation change
Uses gradient of one solute as energy source to transport another solute
Secondary active transport

Na+/GLUCOSE SYMPORTER
Na+ gradient used to move glucose against gradient
